fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 74 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch `if not clamp` should be False, meaning clamping logic executes only when clamp is True — the negation is wrong
The then-branch of `if not clamp` executes clamping; if we reach this branch, clamp must be False — but the failing tests pass clamp=True, so this branch should not be entered when clamp=True

spec.expr: y__2 >= min(y0, y1) and y__2 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After clamping, y must be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]

spec.expr: not clamp or (__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the returned value must be restricted to [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]
